title: dactylorhiza cordigera subsp. siculorum
intro: Dactylorhiza cordigera subsp. siculorum, commonly known as the Balkan marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Southeastern Europe, characterized by its white flowers with a yellow labellum and a growth habit in marshy areas. Traditionally, the Balkan marsh orchid has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as wound healing, reducing inflammation, and treating respiratory issues. The Balkan marsh orchid can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
